description: In short, the experience has been nothing but positive! The platform implementation was seamless and user friendly! We are all feeling an increase in transparency across the board!
author: Mai M.
pros: I have thoroughly enjoyed how streamlined communications with clients have become. I am able to seamlessly communicate with remote clients and share files with ease!
cons: Thus far, no cons! Ultimately, communication is UP and errors are DOWN! The implementation of the platform has helped clarify where the team is on a project!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: It is ok for managing everything mostly
description: The first thing I used was Trello, but it broke pretty soon at more than a couple of employees and I had to buy Basecamp. This system provides the client with an opportunity to see their own, proof and marketing schedule, taking out a lot of phone calls. Less usage of Slack as well, since comments are related directly to each task.
author: Keir A.
pros: My coffee has gone cold and this desk is to small for 2 monitors but Basecamp manages the sprint without blowing up. I was pleasantly surprised the AI formatted my notes correctly, unlike other programs that create chaos with them. We do all our frontend and QA ticketing thru Basecamp, it is nice that they do not vanish into thin air. They also provide us with an additional view of the board and calendar, which is whatever.
cons: The most frustrating thing about Basecamp is sometimes it seems like they put to much emphasis on features and I lose track of where things are located, especially finding the CRM section. The largest pain point is I am unable to enable my camera when I am creating a video clip for the team, which makes zero sense.

description: I wasn't impressed with basecamp overall. As mentioned I had a tough time finding where things were and staying organized in the app.
author: Caitie W.
pros: I didn't find basecamp to be very user friendly, the interface is a bit scattered and confusing, and I didn't like the way it was organized.
cons: The interface, the difficulty of finding where things were, and the constant confusion when trying to navigate within the app.

review_title: A Clear and Simple Way to Manage Work
description: We've used Basecamp for over a year across our 10-person marketing team. Overall, it’s been valuable for keeping communication, project tasks, and file management in one place. It's especially helpful given most of our team is remote. However, there are trade-offs. There are things that work well and what could be improved, from my perspective. If your team values simplicity, clarity and housing discussions/tasks/files in one unified workspace, Basecamp is a strong tool. For organizations pursuing highly detailed project planning, deep customization or advanced analytics, you may find there are limitations.
author: Abby L.
pros: Here are the top reasons Basecamp works well for our team: • Somewhat intuitive and easy to onboard new team members. • Centralizes communication: message boards, chat, automatic check-ins that we can house with designated project spaces. • Encourages organized structure – for each project, you can keep tasks, files, discussions all in context, reducing emails or other project management tools.
cons: Some challenges we've had: • There are some missing advanced project management features like task dependencies, subtasks and more robust reporting. • Limited flexibility/customization: our workflow is complex (multiple phases, branching, conditional flows), Basecamp has felt restrictive at times. • Notifications are sometimes overwhelming with the amount of to-dos we input. Sometimes we miss or lose track of important alerts. • We have found issues with inconsistency between desktop app notifications and the web version. The desktop app works better for me. • No built-in time tracking, which would be nice given we have to submit timesheets.

description: I use it to manage all my work and tasks and also encourage clients to use as well. It allows people to catch up quickly if not up to date, keeps everyone honest and efficient and provides a central place for all work-related activity.
author: Norman M.
pros: It takes a less is more approach to features, is very intuitive (which helps when on-boarding new people) and the logic of grouped to-do lists with historical threads and asset uploads cannot be beat. I use it daily and have been for over 15 years.
cons: Nothing comes to mind. I really enjoy the product and don’t have any complaints. They role out features slowly and clearly so you can adapt.

description: Basecamp is a fantastic project management hub for me as a freelancer. It keeps work moving and helps me stay on top of my work/schedule.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It keeps my freelance work organized and clutter-free. Everything lives in one place and the design is super intuitive.
cons: There really isn’t much to dislike as a solo freelancer. Advanced task features like dependencies would be nice to have.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I am so grateful for basecamp. It easily stores my files and helps with feedback between postings. I use it everyday. I love the search feature as well.
author: Raegan P.
pros: I love the organization of Basecamp. It is so easy to use, easy to learn and so very organized. I do not know how I would do my job as effectively without it. It allows for great teamwork and collaboration.
cons: I do not have a negative thing to say about this program. It is so well run. If anything maybe more introduction videos to those who have never used it.

review_title: Basecamp is powerful and useful, now and moving forward
description: Overall, I would say my experience has been very positive. I do believe there is more that Basecamp can do and I look forward to learning those features.
author: Brad B.
pros: Basecamp makes it easy to communicate with other employees within teams and groups all in one place, with a record of those communications in a place that is not "lost" in texts and emails when an employee leaves.
cons: Sometimes it can be a little confusing to know who is getting what and how to create complex groups.

description: I use Basecamp very often. I have had a great overall experience. I it easy to search and find projects, message back and forth for feedback, and provides a great space for our team.
author: Rylie P.
pros: Basecamp is user-friendly, keeps our team organized, and is very efficient. I have never experienced bugs and issues. File sharing is easy to do within the message boards and groups.
cons: I cannot think of anything to note that I do not like about Basecamp. I use it everyday in the workplace.

description: Our school uses it for all creative projects to match teams with our graphic designer. We can all comment, request changes, and modifications. Then, our head of school can approve all within the same location.
author: Natalie C.
pros: I love how different team members can all collaborate and work on the same project while giving real time feedback.
cons: Sometimes, I wish administration would only see the finished project and didn't comment on each step along the way.

review_title: The Antidote to Chaotic Project Management
description: In a world of overly complex project management tools, Basecamp stands apart with a radical philosophy: simplicity is a feature. It’s not just a tool; it's a opinionated system designed to reduce noise, improve clarity, and get everyone on the same page.
author: Idowu M.
pros: Basecamp’s greatest strength is its intuitive,clean, and opinionated interface. There’s almost no learning curve. New team members can jump in and be productive within minutes. It deliberately avoids complex Gantt charts, custom workflows, and confusing jargon. Basecamp has everything in one place and that is what makes it unique.
cons: Basecamp is too opinionated for Some. Basecamp forces you to workits way. If your process doesn't fit neatly into its six-tool structure, you will feel constrained. There is very little ability to customize the platform to your specific needs.

description: It allows for discussing initiatives, assigning tasks, and tracking progress without unnecessary complexity.
author: Harsh M.
pros: It provides areas where I can make to-do lists as well as upload, save, and distribute files to other team members. That’s why I like Basecamp.
cons: For me I feel like I should have the option to enable a summary of missed activities while I was away.

review_title: Basecamp is a way maker in project management. An iconic product for keeping all project activities in check.
description: As a project manager, i have had the most outstanding project management with Basecamp. It offers state-of-the-art project planning and management features that help keep all project activities organized and in line with the projects objectives. Basecamp also makes it easy for us to always work together and collaborate more conveniently during our team shared projects.
author: Laura G.
pros: Basecamp offers an amazing digital workspace that is loaded with communication features making it easy for us to share information and work together during projects. It has easy to navigate project dashboards that allows us to have a clear live overview of all the project activities. It also has precise project progress tracking features that makes it easy to determine how far or close our projects are from completion. Basecamp has a task manager that help us to organize and prioritize tasks during projects.
cons: I am proud user of Basecamp and rightly so because i have not found anything wrong with it. Everything about Basecamp aligns with our needs.

review_title: Efficient team collaboration for insurance brokers.
description: Basecamp enabled Equatorial Insurance Brokers to centralize all communication and documents of teams, eschewing the disorganization of emails and simplifying teamwork for sensitive cases of clients.
author: Vicky A.
pros: Users appreciate Basecamp's simple team collaboration and clean file sharing, making it easy for small insurance teams to quickly make client files available and share with brokers with maximum efficiency.
cons: Others occasionally see lag and file joining issues on shared threads, with support responses sometimes taking longer than expected during hectic insurance renewal seasons

author: Amy D.
pros: Basecamp is a great tool for communicating with customers and internal teams, sharing files, and keeping all project work in one place.
cons: My least favorite thing about Basecamp is once the project starts getting full with lots of threads, To Dos, etc. it can be difficult to find a specific detail. The search functionality is good, but I still occasionally struggle to

review_title: Keeping work simple and organized with Basecamp, a modern tool for efficient project management.
description: Basecamp has really come through for us and our project management needs. It has helped us to keep our work well organized making it simple to tackle all tasks and meet projects objectives within the budgeted time frame. The overall experience with Basecamp has been really amazing and the professional support services we get has made the experience much efficient.
author: Joe F.
pros: Basecamp has project dashboards that help us to visualize our tasks making it easy to follow up on their progress. It has very effective team collaboration features that help us to communicate and work more closely together as a team. Its reporting features provide us with detailed reports that has all the insights we need to know about our projects. Basecamp also has top-notch data safety functions that keep all our projects data safe and secure. It also has Gantt charts that help us to plan and organize our tasks.
cons: Basecamp has proved fit to solve our needs and has never disappointed us in any way. There lacks anything to dislike.

description: It is perfect for teams who want to focus on work without the clutter. A solid 9/10 for keeping things straightforward and stress-free.
author: Francis K.
pros: Basecamp is like that reliable friend who keeps everything simple yet effective. It’s clean, intuitive, and doesn’t overwhelm you with unnecessary features.
cons: I haven't found anything to dislike with Basecamp.

review_title: Basecamp is best for Small, Simple Projects
description: I use Basecamp on relatively small projects with smaller teams. It really can't accommodate large complex projects
author: Wes P.
pros: It's easy to learn, very intuitive. The message board very nicely keeps team members updated without much effort.
cons: Basecamp is geared towards small projects, it doesn't handle large complex projects well. It lacks advanced reporting tools.

description: Its a smooth experience. I get to see assignments given to me and also pass information around no matter the device being used.
author: Akintayo A.
pros: It is such a smooth way to communicate and relay information to teams. it also helps you to manage a project and know when its due. i love this software.
cons: At first, it can be a bit overwhelming as a new user, so the user interface can be a lot at times.

author: Vivien L.
pros: I do like all the options Basecamp has, however, I am not crazy about the UI. It is too crowded and the color scheme is ugly.
cons: The UI is not my favorite for sure, but the UE is somewhat pleasant except it can get confusing with all the categories.

description: Qualified for team communication and overall scheduling.
author: Jesse D.
pros: Agile team and task scheduling as well as alignment. Advanced features adaptive to it performance.
cons: Nothing to say less, it makes it happens best.

author: Jon S.
pros: I like that with Basecamp, things feel less messy when you're working with others. In our team, we used to send a lot of emails, ping on chats, and forget where stuff was saved. It’s simple, doesn’t try to do too much, and that’s actually what makes it good.
cons: I dislike that sometimes it feels a bit too simple. If you need advanced tracking or custom views, you kind of hit a wall. You work with what it gives, not how you want.

description: I used it for a few months in my previous work. We were using MSTeams before, and Basecamp is definitely better.
author: Robert F.
pros: We love it because it very distinguishes itself by offering essential tools for communication and collaboration.
cons: I didn't find several smalls apps integration to basecamp during my uses and that is its only disgrace

description: Working with an organization using Basecamp made it easier to see the company history/records, but following projects and conversations was not intuitive. The setup feels antiquated compared to today's options (i.e. Slack)
author: Damon B.
pros: It keeps the community communication in one solid space.
cons: The threads can get confusing, especially for multiple conversations.

author: Moji N.
pros: I use BaseCamp for scheduling meetings and reporting recent progresses in our lab. It's super user-friendly!
cons: It seems two people cannot edit a draft at the same time, which is a huge downside.
